content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

attachment.content:
- docs-updater: Docs Updater Agent
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Development Guide
2	
3	This guide covers everything you need to know to contribute to SwissKit, from setting up your development environment to
4	implementing new features.
5	
6	## Table of Contents
7	
8	- [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
9	- [Setting Up Development Environment](#setting-up-development-environment)
10	- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
11	- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
12	- [Adding New Tools](#adding-new-tools)
13	- [Testing](#testing)
14	- [Building](#building)
15	- [Common Tasks](#common-tasks)
16	
17	## Prerequisites
18	
19	Before you start developing, ensure you have:
20	
21	- **JDK 11 or higher**
22	- **Maven 3.6 or higher**
23	- **IntelliJ IDEA** (recommended) or your preferred IDE
24	- **Git**
25	
26	### Verify Installation
27	
28	```bash
29	# Check Java version
30	java -version
31	
32	# Check Maven version
33	mvn -version
34	
35	# Check Git version
36	git --version
37	```
38	
39	## Setting Up Development Environment
40	
41	### 1. Clone the Repository
42	
43	```bash
44	git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
45	cd SwissKit
46	```
47	
48	### 2. Import into IDE
49	
50	**IntelliJ IDEA**:
51	
52	1. Open IntelliJ IDEA
53	2. Select "Open" and choose the project directory
54	3. Wait for Maven to download dependencies
55	4. The project will be automatically recognized as a Maven project
56	
57	### 3. Build the Project
58	
59	```bash
60	mvn clean compile
61	```
62	
63	### 4. Run the Application
64	
65	```bash
66	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
67	```
68	
69	Or run `Main.java` directly from your IDE.
70	
71	## Project Structure
72	
73	```
74	SwissKit/
75	├── src/main/java/fan/summer/
76	│   ├── Main.java                    # Entry point
77	│   ├── annoattion/                  # Annotations
78	│   │   └── SwissKitPage.java       # Page annotation
79	│   ├── kitpage/                     # Tool modules
80	│   │   ├── KitPage.java            # Interface
81	│   │   ├── KitPageScanner.java     # Auto-discovery scanner
82	│   │   ├── WelcomePage.java        # Welcome page
83	│   │   ├── excel/                  # Excel tool
84	│   │   │   ├── listener/           # Event listeners
85	│   │   │   └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/             # Background workers
86	│   │   └── email/                  # Email tool
87	│   ├── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/                         # Custom UI components
88	│   │   └── components/
89	│   └── utils/                      # Utilities
90	├── src/main/resources/              # Resources (icons, etc.)
91	├── pom.xml                          # Maven configuration
92	└── docs/                            # Documentation
93	```
94	
95	## Code Standards
96	
97	### Naming Conventions
98	
99	**Packages**:
100	
101	- Tool pages: `fan.summer.kitpage.{toolName}`
102	- Listeners: `listener/{function}Listener.java`
103	- Workers: `plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/{function}Worker.java`
104	- Callbacks: `plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/{function}Callback.java`
105	
106	**Classes**:
107	
108	- Pages: `{ToolName}KitPage` or `{ToolName}Page`
109	- Listeners: `{Function}Listener`
110	- Workers: `{Function}Worker`
111	- Callbacks: `{Function}Callback`
112	
113	**Methods and Variables**:
114	
115	- Use camelCase
116	- Be descriptive and concise
117	
118	```java
119	// Good
120	public void analyzeExcelFile(Path filePath) { }
121	private int currentProgress;
122	
123	// Bad
124	public void analyzeExcel() { }
125	private int cp;
126	```
127	
128	### Documentation
129	
130	- Add Javadoc for all public methods
131	- Use clear, concise descriptions
132	- Include parameters and return values
133	
134	```java
135	/**
136	 * Analyzes an Excel file and extracts header information.
137	 *
138	 * @param filePath the path to the Excel file to analyze
139	 * @return a map of sheet names to their headers
140	 * @throws IOException if the file cannot be read
141	 */
142	public Map<String, Map<Integer, String>> analyzeExcel(Path filePath) throws IOException {
143	    // Implementation
144	}
145	```
146	
147	### Language
148	
149	- All code comments must be in **English**
150	- All UI text must be in **English**
151	- Variable and method names in English
152	
153	### UI Standards
154	
155	- Use `SansSerif` font for UI components
156	- Use color constants from `UIUtils`
157	- Recommend layouts: `BorderLayout`, `GridBagLayout`, `BoxLayout`
158	
159	```java
160	JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
161	titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
162	panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
163	```
164	
165	## Adding New Tools
166	
167	### Step-by-Step Guide
168	
169	**1. Create Package Structure**
170	
171	```bash
172	mkdir -p src/main/java/fan/summer/kitpage/mytool
173	```
174	
175	**2. Implement KitPage Interface with @SwissKitPage Annotation**
176	
177	```java
178	package fan.summer.kitpage.mytool;
179	
180	import fan.summer.api.KitPage;
181	import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage;
182	
183	import javax.swing.*;
184	import java.awt.*;
185	
186	@SwissKitPage(
187	        menuName = "🔧 My Tool",
188	        menuTooltip = "Open My Tool",
189	        visible = true,
190	        order = 10
191	)
192	public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
193	    private JPanel panel;
194	
195	    public MyToolPage() {
196	        initComponents();
197	    }
198	
199	    private void initComponents() {
200	        panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
201	
202	        // Add your UI components here
203	        JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
204	        titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
205	
206	        panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
207	    }
208	
209	    @Override
210	    public JPanel getPanel() {
211	        return panel;
212	    }
213	}
214	```
215	
216	**Annotation Properties**:
217	
218	| Property      | Type    | Default | Description                   |
219	|---------------|---------|---------|-------------------------------|
220	| `menuName`    | String  | ""      | Display name in sidebar       |
221	| `menuTooltip` | String  | ""      | Tooltip on hover              |
222	| `visible`     | boolean | true    | Whether to show in menu       |
223	| `order`       | int     | 0       | Display order (lower = first) |
224	
225	**3. Build and Test**
226	
227	```bash
228	mvn clean compile
229	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
230	```
231	
232	Your new tool will appear automatically in the sidebar!
233	
234	### Adding Background Processing
235	
236	For time-consuming operations, use SwingWorker:
237	
238	```java
239	public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
240	    private JProgressBar progressBar;
241	    private JButton processButton;
242	
243	    public MyToolPage() {
244	        initComponents();
245	    }
246	
247	    private void initComponents() {
248	        panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
249	
250	        // Create components
251	        progressBar = new GradientProgressBar();
252	        processButton = new JButton("Process");
253	
254	        // Add action listener
255	        processButton.addActionListener(e -> {
256	            new MyWorker(progressBar, processButton, panel).execute();
257	        });
258	
259	        // Layout
260	        panel.add(progressBar, BorderLayout.CENTER);
261	        panel.add(processButton, BorderLayout.SOUTH);
262	    }
263	}
264	
265	class MyWorker extends SwingWorker<Void, Integer> {
266	    private final JProgressBar progressBar;
267	    private final JButton button;
268	    private final Component parent;
269	
270	    public MyWorker(JProgressBar progressBar, JButton button, Component parent) {
271	        this.progressBar = progressBar;
272	        this.button = button;
273	        this.parent = parent;
274	    }
275	
276	    @Override
277	    protected Void doInBackground() throws Exception {
278	        SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> {
279	            progressBar.setValue(0);
280	            progressBar.setStringPainted(true);
281	            button.setEnabled(false);
282	        });
283	
284	        // Do work here
285	        for (int i = 0; i <= 100; i++) {
286	            Thread.sleep(50);
287	            publish(i);
288	        }
289	
290	        return null;
291	    }
292	
293	    @Override
294	    protected void process(List<Integer> chunks) {
295	        if (!chunks.isEmpty()) {
296	            int latest = chunks.get(chunks.size() - 1);
297	            progressBar.setValue(latest);
298	            progressBar.setString("Processing... " + latest + "%");
299	        }
300	    }
301	
302	    @Override
303	    protected void done() {
304	        try {
305	            get();
306	            progressBar.setValue(100);
307	            progressBar.setString("Completed!");
308	            button.setEnabled(true);
309	        } catch (Exception e) {
310	            progressBar.setString("Failed: " + e.getMessage());
311	            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent, e.getMessage(), "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
312	            button.setEnabled(true);
313	        }
314	    }
315	}
316	```
317	
318	## Testing
319	
320	### Running Tests
321	
322	```bash
323	mvn test
324	```
325	
326	### Writing Tests
327	
328	```java
329	import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
330	import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
331	
332	class ExcelKitPageTest {
333	
334	    @Test
335	    void testGetTitle() {
336	        ExcelKitPage page = new ExcelKitPage();
337	        assertEquals("Excel", page.getTitle());
338	    }
339	}
340	```
341	
342	## Building
343	
344	### Clean Build
345	
346	```bash
347	mvn clean package
348	```
349	
350	### Skip Tests
351	
352	```bash
353	mvn clean package -DskipTests
354	```
355	
356	### Create Executable JAR
357	
358	```bash
359	mvn clean package
360	# Output: target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
361	```
362	
363	### Run Executable JAR
364	
365	```bash
366	java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
367	```
368	
369	## Common Tasks
370	
371	### Adding a Dependency
372	
373	Edit `pom.xml`:
374	
375	```xml
376	<dependency>
377	    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
378	    <artifactId>library-name</artifactId>
379	    <version>1.0.0</version>
380	</dependency>
381	```
382	
383	Then run:
384	
385	```bash
386	mvn dependency:resolve
387	```
388	
389	### Adding an Icon
390	
391	Place icon in `src/main/resources/`:
392	
393	- `icon.png` (preferred)
394	- `icon.jpg`
395	- `app.png`
396	
397	### Debugging
398	
399	Enable verbose logging:
400	
401	```bash
402	java -Dlog4j.configurationFile=path/to/log4j2.xml -jar target/SwissKit-1.1.0.jar
403	```
404	
405	Or add logging to your code:
406	
407	```java
408	private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyClass.class);
409	
410	logger.info("Processing started");
411	logger.error("Error occurred", exception);
412	```
413	
414	## Best Practices
415	
416	### Thread Safety
417	
418	- Always update UI components in EDT thread
419	- Use `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()` for UI updates from background threads
420	
421	```java
422	// Wrong - from background thread
423	progressBar.setValue(50);
424	
425	// Correct
426	SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> progressBar.setValue(50));
427	```
428	
429	### Error Handling
430	
431	- Always handle exceptions gracefully
432	- Provide user-friendly error messages
433	- Log errors for debugging
434	
435	```java
436	try {
437	    // Operation
438	} catch (Exception e) {
439	    logger.error("Operation failed", e);
440	    JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent,
441	            "Operation failed: " + e.getMessage(),
442	            "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
443	}
444	```
445	
446	### Resource Management
447	
448	- Use try-with-resources for file operations
449	- Close resources properly
450	
451	```java
452	try (Workbook workbook = WorkbookFactory.create(file)) {
453	    // Use workbook
454	} // Automatically closed
455	```
456	
457	## Contributing
458	
459	See the [Contributing Guide](contributing.md) for guidelines on contributing to SwissKit.
460	
461	---
462	
463	**Ready to contribute?** Check out [Contributing](contributing.md) for submission guidelines!

1	# Architecture
2	
3	SwissKit is built with a modular, plugin-based architecture that allows for easy extension and maintenance. This section
4	covers the core architectural patterns and design decisions.
5	
6	## Table of Contents
7	
8	- [Overview](#overview)
9	- [Project Modules](#project-modules)
10	- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
11	- [Plugin System](#plugin-system)
12	- [Database Layer](#database-layer)
13	- [UI Components](#plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui-components)
14	- [Background Processing](#background-processing)
15	- [Excel Processing](#excel-processing)
16	- [Technology Stack](#technology-stack)
17	
18	## Overview
19	
20	SwissKit follows these architectural principles:
21	
22	- **Modularity** - Each tool is a self-contained module
23	- **Extensibility** - Easy to add new tools via plugins
24	- **Performance** - Efficient processing with streaming and async operations
25	- **Maintainability** - Clean code structure and separation of concerns
26	- **Reusability** - Shared components in SwissKitJ-Api module
27	
28	## Project Modules
29	
30	SwissKit uses a multi-module Maven structure:
31	
32	### SwissKitJ-Api Module
33	
34	The API module provides shared components for both the core application and external plugins:
35	
36	```
37	SwissKitJ-Api/
38	├── src/main/java/fan/summer/
39	│   ├── annoattion/
40	│   │   └── SwissKitPage.java        # Page annotation
41	│   ├── api/
42	│   │   └── KitPage.java             # Plugin interface
43	│   └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/
44	│       ├── GradientProgressBar.java # Custom progress bar
45	│       └── FixedWidthComboBox.java  # Fixed-width combo box
46	└── pom.xml
47	```
48	
49	**Key Features:**
50	
51	- Generates source JAR and Javadoc JAR during package phase
52	- Only depends on Lombok (minimal dependencies)
53	- Must be installed to local Maven repository before building the main module
54	
55	**Dependency for Plugins:**
56	
57	```xml
58	<dependency>
59	    <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
60	    <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
61	    <version>2.0.1</version>
62	</dependency>
63	```
64	
65	### Main Module (SwissKit)
66	
67	The core application with Excel, Email, and Settings tools.
68	
69	### SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc
70	
71	Example plugin project demonstrating plugin development.
72	
73	### SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning
74	
75	Auto-learning plugin demonstrating isolated plugin classloading with Java 11 HttpClient and fastjson2.
76	
77	## Project Structure
78	
79	```
80	SwissKit/
81	├── SwissKitJ-Api/                   # Shared API module
82	│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
83	│       ├── annoattion/
84	│       │   └── SwissKitPage.java    # Page annotation
85	│       ├── api/
86	│       │   └── KitPage.java         # Plugin interface
87	│       └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/
88	│           ├── GradientProgressBar.java
89	│           └── FixedWidthComboBox.java
90	├── src/main/java/fan/summer/
91	│   ├── Main.java                    # Application entry point
92	│   ├── database/                    # Database layer
93	│   │   ├── DatabaseInit.java        # Database initialization
94	│   │   ├── SwissKitDBTable.java     # Table marker interface
95	│   │   ├── entity/                  # Entity classes
96	│   │   │   ├── email/
97	│   │   │   │   ├── EmailMassSentConfigEntity.java
98	│   │   │   │   └── EmailSentLogEntity.java
99	│   │   │   ├── excel/
100	│   │   │   │   └── ComplexSplitConfigEntity.java
101	│   │   │   └── setting/

103	│   │   │       ├── EmailAddressBookEntity.java

105	│   │   ├── mapper/                  # MyBatis mappers
106	│   │   │   ├── email/

215	│   │           ├── WebUtil.java          # HTTP client with gzip support
216	│   │           └── ConfigLoader.java      # JSON config loader
217	│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc/             # Example plugin
218	```
219	
220	## Plugin System
221	
222	The plugin system is the core of SwissKit's extensibility.
223	
224	### @SwissKitPage Annotation
225	
226	Located in `SwissKitJ-Api` module at `fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage`:
227	
228	```java
229	@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME)
230	@Target(ElementType.TYPE)
231	public @interface SwissKitPage {
232	    String menuName() default "";
233	    String menuTooltip() default "";
234	    boolean visible() default true;
235	    int order() default 0;
236	}
237	```
238	
239	### KitPage Interface
240	
241	Located in `SwissKitJ-Api` module at `fan.summer.api.KitPage`:
242	
243	```java
244	public interface KitPage {
245	    JPanel getPanel();              // Get page panel
246	    default String getMenuName() {  // Get menu display name (from annotation)
247	        return "";
248	    }
249	    default Icon getMenuIcon() {    // Get menu icon (optional)
250	        return null;
251	    }
252	    default String getMenuTooltip() { // Get menu tooltip (from annotation)
253	        return "";
254	    }
255	}
256	```
257	
258	### SwissKitPageScanner
259	
260	The `SwissKitPageScaner` class handles automatic discovery of all tool pages using SPI:
261	
262	```java
263	public class SwissKitPageScaner {
264	    /**
265	     * Scans for KitPage implementations using SPI ServiceLoader.
266	     *
267	     * @return list of visible KitPage instances sorted by order
268	     */
269	    public static List<KitPage> scan() {
270	        // 1. Load services from META-INF/services/
271	        // 2. Filter by @SwissKitPage visible = true
272	        // 3. Sort by order() value
273	        // 4. Instantiate and return
274	    }
275	}
276	```
277	
278	### Auto-Discovery Mechanism
279	
280	SwissKit automatically discovers and loads all tools at runtime using SPI:
281	
282	**Discovery Method**: Java SPI (Service Provider Interface)
283	
284	**SPI Service File**: `src/main/resources/META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
285	
286	**Current Registered Pages**:
287	
288	```
289	fan.summer.kitpage.welcome.WelcomePage
290	fan.summer.kitpage.excel.ExcelKitPage
291	fan.summer.kitpage.email.EmailKitPage
292	fan.summer.kitpage.setting.SettingKitPage
293	```
294	
295	### External Plugin Loading
296	
297	SwissKit supports loading external JAR plugins:
298	
299	**Plugin Directory**: `.swisskit/plugins/`
300	
301	**Loading Process**:
302	
303	1. `PluginLoader` scans plugin directory at startup
304	2. Loads JAR files using `IsolatedPluginClassLoader` (extends URLClassLoader with parent=null)
305	3. Scans for `KitPage` implementations via SPI ServiceLoader
306	4. Registers discovered pages
307	
308	**IsolatedPluginClassLoader Strategy**:
309	
310	The `IsolatedPluginClassLoader` implements isolated classloading with a break-parent-delegation strategy:
311	
312	| Class Pattern          | Loading Strategy                                      |
313	|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
314	| `fan.summer.*`         | Delegate to main app ClassLoader (shared interfaces)  |
315	| `java.*`, `javax.*`    | Delegate to main app ClassLoader (JDK modules)         |
316	| `sun.*`, `com.sun.*`   | Delegate to main app ClassLoader (JDK internals)      |
317	| Other classes          | Try main app ClassLoader first → plugin JAR → fallback |
318	
319	This ensures:
320	- Plugin classes (DTOs, services, utils) are loaded from plugin JAR
321	- Third-party libraries (fastjson2, HttpClient) are accessible from plugin JAR
322	- `fan.summer.*` interfaces/annotations use shared Class objects for correct instanceof behavior
323	- Classes in plugin JAR are found even when called via `Class.forName()` from third-party library internals
324	
325	## Database Layer
326	
327	SwissKit uses H2 as embedded database with MyBatis for data access.
328	
329	### Database Location
330	
331	- **Path**: `.swisskit/swisskit.db` (relative to application runtime directory)
332	- **Type**: H2 (embedded, file-based, no external server required)
333	
334	### Database Initialization
335	
336	The `DatabaseInit` class handles:
337	
338	- Creating `.swisskit` directory if not exists
339	- Creating database tables via `init.sql`
340	- Initializing MyBatis SqlSessionFactory
341	
342	```java
343	// Initialize database
344	DatabaseInit.init();
345	
346	// Get SqlSession for CRUD operations
347	try (SqlSession session = DatabaseInit.getSqlSession()) {
348	    MyMapper mapper = session.getMapper(MyMapper.class);
349	    // perform operations
350	    session.commit();
351	}
352	```
353	
354	### Database Tables
355	
356	| Table Name                | Purpose                                    |
357	|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
358	| `swiss_kit_setting_email` | Email SMTP configuration                   |
359	| `complex_split_config`    | Excel complex split configuration          |
360	| `email_address_book`      | Email contacts with nicknames and tags     |
361	| `email_tag`               | Tags for categorizing contacts             |
362	| `email_mass_sent_config`  | Mass email sending configuration           |
363	| `email_sent_log`          | Email sending history with status tracking |
364	
365	### MyBatis Configuration
366	
367	Database configuration is in `src/main/resources/mybatis-config.xml`:
368	
369	```xml
370	<environment id="swisskit">
371	    <transactionManager type="JDBC"/>
372	    <dataSource type="UNPOOLED">
373	        <property name="driver" value="org.h2.Driver"/>
374	        <property name="url" value="${db.url}"/>
375	    </dataSource>
376	</environment>
377	```
378	
379	### Entity Pattern
380	
381	All entities use Lombok for boilerplate reduction:
382	
383	```java
384	@Data
385	public class EmailAddressBookEntity {
386	    private Integer id;
387	    private String emailAddress;
388	    private String nickname;
389	    private String tags;  // JSON array of tag names
390	}
391	```
392	
393	## UI Components
394	
395	SwissKit includes custom UI components for a modern, consistent appearance. These components are located in the
396	`SwissKitJ-Api` module.
397	
398	### SideMenuBar
399	
400	Dynamic side menu with the following architecture:
401	
402	```java
403	public class SideMenuBar extends JPanel {
404	    private static final int MENU_WIDTH = 160;
405	    private List<KitPage> pages;
406	    private int selectedIndex = 0;
407	
408	    // Color scheme
409	    private static final Color SELECTED_BG = new Color(0x2D, 0x2D, 0x2D);
410	    private static final Color SELECTED_TEXT = new Color(0xBB, 0x86, 0xFC);
411	    private static final Color HOVER_BG = new Color(0xE8, 0xE8, 0xE8);
412	}
413	```
414	
415	**Features**:
416	
417	- Dynamic menu item generation
418	- Selected state highlighting
419	- Mouse hover effects
420	- Custom icons and tooltips
421	- Runtime page management
422	
423	### GradientProgressBar
424	
425	Custom progress bar with animation (located in `SwissKitJ-Api` module):
426	
427	```java
428	public class GradientProgressBar extends JProgressBar {
429	    private Timer animationTimer;
430	    private float animatedValue = 0f;
431	    private float targetValue = 0f;
432	
433	    public GradientProgressBar() {
434	        setOpaque(false);
435	        // 60 FPS animation
436	        animationTimer = new Timer(16, e -> {
437	            // Easing effect
438	            animatedValue += (targetValue - animatedValue) * 0.08f;
439	            repaint();
440	        });
441	        animationTimer.start();
442	    }
443	}
444	```
445	
446	**Features**:
447	
448	- Blue to purple gradient
449	- Smooth easing animation (60 FPS)
450	- Rounded corners
451	- Glossy highlight effect
452	
453	### TableUtil
454	
455	Utility class for consistent JTable initialization:
456	
457	```java
458	public abstract class TableUtil {
459	    public static JTable initTable(JTable table, String[] columns, 
460	                                    List<Object[]> rowData, int isCellEditableIndex) {
461	        DefaultTableModel model = new DefaultTableModel(columns, 0) {
462	            @Override
463	            public boolean isCellEditable(int row, int column) {
464	                return column > isCellEditableIndex;
465	            }
466	        };
467	        // ... populate model
468	        return table;
469	    }
470	}
471	```
472	
473	## Background Processing
474	
475	SwissKit uses SwingWorker for all time-consuming operations to avoid blocking the UI.
476	
477	### SwingWorker Pattern
478	
479	```java
480	public class QueryAllEmailInfoWorker extends SwingWorker<List<EmailAddressBookEntity>, Void> {
481	    private QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack callback;
482	
483	    @Override
484	    protected List<EmailAddressBookEntity> doInBackground() throws Exception {
485	        try (SqlSession session = DatabaseInit.getSqlSession()) {
486	            EmailAddressBookMapper mapper = session.getMapper(EmailAddressBookMapper.class);
487	            return mapper.selectEmailAddressBook();
488	        } catch (Exception e) {
489	            return Collections.emptyList();
490	        }
491	    }
492	
493	    @Override
494	    protected void done() {
495	        try {
496	            callback.onSuccess(get());
497	        } catch (Exception e) {
498	            callback.onFailure(e);
499	        }
500	    }
501	}
502	```
503	
504	**Key Benefits**:
505	
506	- Non-blocking UI
507	- Real-time progress updates
508	- Proper error handling
509	- Thread-safe UI updates via `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()`
510	
511	## Excel Processing
512	
513	SwissKit uses Apache FESOD for efficient Excel processing.
514	
515	### Processing Flow
516	
517	```java
518	// 1. Get sheet names
519	try (ExcelReader excelReader = FesodSheet.read(file).build()) {
520	    for (String sheetName : sheetNames) {
521	        HeaderListener headerListener = new HeaderListener();
522	        ReadSheet readSheet = FesodSheet.readSheet(sheetName)
523	                .headRowNumber(1)
524	                .registerReadListener(headerListener)
525	                .build();
526	        excelReader.read(readSheet);
527	    }
528	}
529	```
530	
531	### Listeners
532	
533	**HeaderListener** - Extracts headers from first row:
534	
535	```java
536	public class HeaderListener extends AnalysisEventListener<Map<Integer, String>> {
537	    private Map<Integer, String> headers = new HashMap<>();
538	
539	    @Override
540	    public void invokeHeadMap(Map<Integer, String> headMap, AnalysisContext context) {
541	        this.headers = headMap;
542	    }
543	}
544	```
545	
546	**NoModelDataListener** - Streams all data with logging:
547	
548	```java
549	@Slf4j
550	public class NoModelDataListener extends AnalysisEventListener<Map<Integer, Object>> {
551	    private List<Map<Integer, Object>> cachedDataList;
552	
553	    @Override
554	    public void invoke(Map<Integer, Object> data, AnalysisContext context) {
555	        log.info("Parsed one data row: {}", JSON.toJSONString(data));
556	        cachedDataList.add(data);
557	    }
558	}
559	```
560	
561	## Technology Stack
562	
563	### Core Technologies
564	
565	- **Language**: Java 11
566	- **Build Tool**: Maven 3.6+
567	- **UI Framework**: Swing
568	- **UI Theme**: FlatLaf 3.5
569	- **Database**: H2 2.4.240 + MyBatis 3.5.19
570	
571	### Dependencies
572	
573	| Dependency        | Version          | Purpose                |
574	|-------------------|------------------|------------------------|
575	| flatlaf           | 3.5              | Modern UI theme        |
576	| fesod-sheet       | 2.0.1-incubating | Excel streaming        |
577	| log4j-core        | 2.25.4           | Logging                |
578	| log4j-slf4j2-impl | 2.25.4           | SLF4J binding          |
579	| slf4j-api         | 2.0.16           | Logging API            |
580	| lombok            | 1.18.42          | Code simplification    |
581	| fastjson2         | 2.0.59           | JSON processing        |
582	| h2                | 2.4.240          | H2 Database            |
583	| mybatis           | 3.5.19           | MyBatis ORM            |
584	| miglayout-swing   | 11.3             | Layout manager         |
585	| simple-java-mail  | 8.12.6           | Email sending via SMTP |
586	
587	## Design Patterns
588	
589	SwissKit employs several design patterns:
590	
591	### Plugin Pattern
592	
593	```java
594	public interface KitPage { }
595	public class ExcelPage implements KitPage { }
596	```
597	
598	### Observer Pattern
599	
600	```java
601	public interface QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack {
602	    void onSuccess(List<EmailAddressBookEntity> result);
603	    void onFailure(Exception e);
604	}
605	```
606	
607	### Factory Pattern
608	
609	```java
610	public class UIUtils {
611	    public static JPanel createSectionPanel(String title, JComponent content) { }
612	    public static JLabel createPageTitle(String title) { }
613	}
614	```
615	
616	### Module Pattern
617	
618	```java
619	// SwissKitJ-Api module provides shared components
620	// Main module depends on API module
621	// Plugins also depend on API module for compatibility
622	```
623	
624	---
625	
626	**Ready to contribute?** Check out the [Development Guide](development.md) to start building!

# Architecture

SwissKit is built with a modular, plugin-based architecture that allows for easy extension and maintenance. This section
covers the core architectural patterns and design decisions.

## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [Project Modules](#project-modules)
- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
- [Plugin System](#plugin-system)
- [Database Layer](#database-layer)
- [UI Components](#plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui-components)
- [Background Processing](#background-processing)
- [Excel Processing](#excel-processing)
- [Technology Stack](#technology-stack)

## Overview

SwissKit follows these architectural principles:

- **Modularity** - Each tool is a self-contained module
- **Extensibility** - Easy to add new tools via plugins
- **Performance** - Efficient processing with streaming and async operations
- **Maintainability** - Clean code structure and separation of concerns
- **Reusability** - Shared components in SwissKitJ-Api module

## Project Modules

SwissKit uses a multi-module Maven structure:

### SwissKitJ-Api Module

The API module provides shared components for both the core application and external plugins:

```
SwissKitJ-Api/
├── src/main/java/fan/summer/
│   ├── annoattion/
│   │   └── SwissKitPage.java        # Page annotation
│   ├── api/
│   │   └── KitPage.java             # Plugin interface
│   └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/
│       ├── GradientProgressBar.java # Custom progress bar
│       └── FixedWidthComboBox.java  # Fixed-width combo box
└── pom.xml
```

**Key Features:**

- Generates source JAR and Javadoc JAR during package phase
- Only depends on Lombok (minimal dependencies)
- Must be installed to local Maven repository before building the main module

**Dependency for Plugins:**

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
    <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
    <version>2.0.1</version>
</dependency>
```

### Main Module (SwissKit)

The core application with Excel, Email, and Settings tools.

### SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc

Example plugin project demonstrating plugin development.

### SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning

Auto-learning plugin demonstrating isolated plugin classloading with Java 11 HttpClient and fastjson2.

## Project Structure

```
SwissKit/
├── SwissKitJ-Api/                   # Shared API module
│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
│       ├── annoattion/
│       │   └── SwissKitPage.java    # Page annotation
│       ├── api/
│       │   └── KitPage.java         # Plugin interface
│       └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/
│           ├── GradientProgressBar.java
│           └── FixedWidthComboBox.java
├── src/main/java/fan/summer/
│   ├── Main.java                    # Application entry point
│   ├── database/                    # Database layer
│   │   ├── DatabaseInit.java        # Database initialization
│   │   ├── SwissKitDBTable.java     # Table marker interface
│   │   ├── entity/                  # Entity classes
│   │   │   ├── email/
│   │   │   │   ├── EmailMassSentConfigEntity.java
│   │   │   │   └── EmailSentLogEntity.java
│   │   │   ├── excel/
│   │   │   │   └── ComplexSplitConfigEntity.java
│   │   │   └── setting/
│   │   │       ├── SwissKitSettingEmailEntity.java
│   │   │       ├── EmailAddressBookEntity.java
│   │   │       └── EmailTagEntity.java
│   │   ├── mapper/                  # MyBatis mappers
│   │   │   ├── email/

│   │   │   │   └── EmailSentLogMapper.java

│   │       ├── second/              # Sub-views
│   │       │   ├── AddAddressView.java
│   │       │   ├── AddAddressView.jfd
│   │       │   ├── EmailAddressBookView.java
│   │       │   ├── EmailAddressBookView.jfd
│   │       │   ├── EmailTagsView.java
│   │       │   └── EmailTagsView.jfd
│   │       └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/second/       # Query workers
│   │           ├── QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack.java
│   │           └── QueryAllEmailInfoWorker.java
│   ├── plugin/                      # Plugin system
│   │   ├── PluginLoader.java        # Plugin JAR loader
│   │   └── PluginDiagnostic.java    # Plugin diagnostics
│   ├── scaner/                      # SPI-based scanner
│   │   └── SwissKitPageScaner.java  # Auto-discovery scanner
│   ├── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/                          # UI components
│   │   ├── StartLoadingPage.java    # Splash screen
│   │   ├── StartLoadingPage.jfd
│   │   ├── home/
│   │   │   └── HomePage.java        # Main window
│   │   └── sidebar/
│   │       └── SideMenuBar.java     # Side menu
│   └── utils/                       # Utilities
│       ├── AppInfo.java             # Application version info
│       ├── EmailUtil.java           # Email utilities
│       ├── ExcelUtil.java           # Excel utilities
│       ├── FileNameUtil.java        # File name utilities
│       ├── StringUtil.java          # String validation utilities
│       ├── UIUtils.java             # UI utilities
│       └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/
│           └── TableUtil.java       # Table initialization utility
└── src/main/resources/
    ├── app.properties               # Application properties
    ├── init.sql                     # Database initialization SQL
    ├── log4j2.xml                   # Log4j configuration
    ├── mybatis-config.xml           # MyBatis configuration
    ├── mapper/                      # MyBatis mapper XMLs
    │   ├── email/
    │   │   ├── EmailMassSentConfigMapper.xml
    │   │   └── EmailSentLogMapper.xml
    │   ├── excel/
    │   │   └── ComplexSplitConfigMapper.xml
    │   └── setting/
    │       ├── SwissKitSettingEmailMapper.xml
    │       ├── EmailAddressBookMapper.xml
    │       └── EmailTagMapper.xml
    └── META-INF/services/           # SPI plugin.swisskit.hpl.service files
        └── fan.summer.api.KitPage
├── OfficalPlugin/
│   ├── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/  # Auto-learning plugin
│   │   └── src/main/java/plugin/swisskit/hpl/
│   │       ├── HappyLearning.java        # Main UI page
│   │       ├── HappyLearningService.java  # Service layer
│   │       ├── HappyLearningWorker.java  # SwingWorker for background tasks
│   │       ├── DevLauncher.java          # Dev launcher
│   │       ├── dto/                       # Data transfer objects
│   │       │   ├── UserSearchResp.java
│   │       │   ├── LessonSearchResp.java
│   │       │   ├── LessonDetailResp.java
│   │       │   ├── EnterLessonResp.java
│   │       │   └── LearnProcess.java
│   │       └── util/
│   │           ├── WebUtil.java          # HTTP client with gzip support
│   │           └── ConfigLoader.java      # JSON config loader
│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc/             # Example plugin
```

## Plugin System

The plugin system is the core of SwissKit's extensibility.

### @SwissKitPage Annotation

Located in `SwissKitJ-Api` module at `fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage`:

```java
@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME)
@Target(ElementType.TYPE)
public @interface SwissKitPage {
    String menuName() default "";
    String menuTooltip() default "";
    boolean visible() default true;
    int order() default 0;
}
```

### KitPage Interface

Located in `SwissKitJ-Api` module at `fan.summer.api.KitPage`:

```java
public interface KitPage {
    JPanel getPanel();              // Get page panel
    default String getMenuName() {  // Get menu display name (from annotation)
        return "";
    }
    default Icon getMenuIcon() {    // Get menu icon (optional)
        return null;
    }
    default String getMenuTooltip() { // Get menu tooltip (from annotation)
        return "";
    }
}
```

### SwissKitPageScanner

The `SwissKitPageScaner` class handles automatic discovery of all tool pages using SPI:

```java
public class SwissKitPageScaner {
    /**
     * Scans for KitPage implementations using SPI ServiceLoader.
     *
     * @return list of visible KitPage instances sorted by order
     */
    public static List<KitPage> scan() {
        // 1. Load services from META-INF/services/
        // 2. Filter by @SwissKitPage visible = true
        // 3. Sort by order() value
        // 4. Instantiate and return
    }
}
```

### Auto-Discovery Mechanism

SwissKit automatically discovers and loads all tools at runtime using SPI:

**Discovery Method**: Java SPI (Service Provider Interface)

**SPI Service File**: `src/main/resources/META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`

**Current Registered Pages**:

```
fan.summer.kitpage.welcome.WelcomePage
fan.summer.kitpage.excel.ExcelKitPage
fan.summer.kitpage.email.EmailKitPage
fan.summer.kitpage.setting.SettingKitPage
```

### External Plugin Loading

SwissKit supports loading external JAR plugins:

**Plugin Directory**: `.swisskit/plugins/`

**Loading Process**:

1. `PluginLoader` scans plugin directory at startup
2. Loads JAR files using `IsolatedPluginClassLoader` (extends URLClassLoader with parent=null)
3. Scans for `KitPage` implementations via SPI ServiceLoader
4. Registers discovered pages

**IsolatedPluginClassLoader Strategy**:

The `IsolatedPluginClassLoader` implements isolated classloading with a break-parent-delegation strategy:

| Class Pattern          | Loading Strategy                                      |
|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| `fan.summer.*`         | Delegate to main app ClassLoader (shared interfaces)  |
| `java.*`, `javax.*`    | Delegate to main app ClassLoader (JDK modules)         |
| `sun.*`, `com.sun.*`   | Delegate to main app ClassLoader (JDK internals)      |
| Other classes          | Try main app ClassLoader first → plugin JAR → fallback |

This ensures:
- Plugin classes (DTOs, services, utils) are loaded from plugin JAR
- Third-party libraries (fastjson2, HttpClient) are accessible from plugin JAR
- `fan.summer.*` interfaces/annotations use shared Class objects for correct instanceof behavior
- Classes in plugin JAR are found even when called via `Class.forName()` from third-party library internals

## Database Layer

SwissKit uses H2 as embedded database with MyBatis for data access.

### Database Location

- **Path**: `.swisskit/swisskit.db` (relative to application runtime directory)
- **Type**: H2 (embedded, file-based, no external server required)

### Database Initialization

The `DatabaseInit` class handles:

- Creating `.swisskit` directory if not exists
- Creating database tables via `init.sql`
- Initializing MyBatis SqlSessionFactory

```java
// Initialize database
DatabaseInit.init();

// Get SqlSession for CRUD operations
try (SqlSession session = DatabaseInit.getSqlSession()) {
    MyMapper mapper = session.getMapper(MyMapper.class);
    // perform operations
    session.commit();
}
```

### Database Tables

| Table Name                | Purpose                                    |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| `swiss_kit_setting_email` | Email SMTP configuration                   |
| `complex_split_config`    | Excel complex split configuration          |
| `email_address_book`      | Email contacts with nicknames and tags     |
| `email_tag`               | Tags for categorizing contacts             |
| `email_mass_sent_config`  | Mass email sending configuration           |
| `email_sent_log`          | Email sending history with status tracking |

### MyBatis Configuration

Database configuration is in `src/main/resources/mybatis-config.xml`:

```xml
<environment id="swisskit">
    <transactionManager type="JDBC"/>
    <dataSource type="UNPOOLED">
        <property name="driver" value="org.h2.Driver"/>
        <property name="url" value="${db.url}"/>
    </dataSource>
</environment>
```

### Entity Pattern

All entities use Lombok for boilerplate reduction:

```java
@Data
public class EmailAddressBookEntity {
    private Integer id;
    private String emailAddress;
    private String nickname;
    private String tags;  // JSON array of tag names
}
```

## UI Components

SwissKit includes custom UI components for a modern, consistent appearance. These components are located in the
`SwissKitJ-Api` module.

### SideMenuBar

Dynamic side menu with the following architecture:

```java
public class SideMenuBar extends JPanel {
    private static final int MENU_WIDTH = 160;
    private List<KitPage> pages;
    private int selectedIndex = 0;

    // Color scheme
    private static final Color SELECTED_BG = new Color(0x2D, 0x2D, 0x2D);
    private static final Color SELECTED_TEXT = new Color(0xBB, 0x86, 0xFC);
    private static final Color HOVER_BG = new Color(0xE8, 0xE8, 0xE8);
}
```

**Features**:

- Dynamic menu item generation
- Selected state highlighting
- Mouse hover effects
- Custom icons and tooltips
- Runtime page management

### GradientProgressBar

Custom progress bar with animation (located in `SwissKitJ-Api` module):

```java
public class GradientProgressBar extends JProgressBar {
    private Timer animationTimer;
    private float animatedValue = 0f;
    private float targetValue = 0f;

    public GradientProgressBar() {
        setOpaque(false);
        // 60 FPS animation
        animationTimer = new Timer(16, e -> {
            // Easing effect
            animatedValue += (targetValue - animatedValue) * 0.08f;
            repaint();
        });
        animationTimer.start();
    }
}
```

**Features**:

- Blue to purple gradient
- Smooth easing animation (60 FPS)
- Rounded corners
- Glossy highlight effect

### TableUtil

Utility class for consistent JTable initialization:

```java
public abstract class TableUtil {
    public static JTable initTable(JTable table, String[] columns, 
                                    List<Object[]> rowData, int isCellEditableIndex) {
        DefaultTableModel model = new DefaultTableModel(columns, 0) {
            @Override
            public boolean isCellEditable(int row, int column) {
                return column > isCellEditableIndex;
            }
        };
        // ... populate model
        return table;
    }
}
```

## Background Processing

SwissKit uses SwingWorker for all time-consuming operations to avoid blocking the UI.

### SwingWorker Pattern

```java
public class QueryAllEmailInfoWorker extends SwingWorker<List<EmailAddressBookEntity>, Void> {
    private QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack callback;

    @Override
    protected List<EmailAddressBookEntity> doInBackground() throws Exception {
        try (SqlSession session = DatabaseInit.getSqlSession()) {
            EmailAddressBookMapper mapper = session.getMapper(EmailAddressBookMapper.class);
            return mapper.selectEmailAddressBook();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            return Collections.emptyList();
        }
    }

    @Override
    protected void done() {
        try {
            callback.onSuccess(get());
        } catch (Exception e) {
            callback.onFailure(e);
        }
    }
}
```

**Key Benefits**:

- Non-blocking UI
- Real-time progress updates
- Proper error handling
- Thread-safe UI updates via `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()`

## Excel Processing

SwissKit uses Apache FESOD for efficient Excel processing.

### Processing Flow

```java
// 1. Get sheet names
try (ExcelReader excelReader = FesodSheet.read(file).build()) {
    for (String sheetName : sheetNames) {
        HeaderListener headerListener = new HeaderListener();
        ReadSheet readSheet = FesodSheet.readSheet(sheetName)
                .headRowNumber(1)
                .registerReadListener(headerListener)
                .build();
        excelReader.read(readSheet);
    }
}
```

### Listeners

**HeaderListener** - Extracts headers from first row:

```java
public class HeaderListener extends AnalysisEventListener<Map<Integer, String>> {
    private Map<Integer, String> headers = new HashMap<>();

    @Override
    public void invokeHeadMap(Map<Integer, String> headMap, AnalysisContext context) {
        this.headers = headMap;
    }
}
```

**NoModelDataListener** - Streams all data with logging:

```java
@Slf4j
public class NoModelDataListener extends AnalysisEventListener<Map<Integer, Object>> {
    private List<Map<Integer, Object>> cachedDataList;

    @Override
    public void invoke(Map<Integer, Object> data, AnalysisContext context) {
        log.info("Parsed one data row: {}", JSON.toJSONString(data));
        cachedDataList.add(data);
    }
}
```

## Technology Stack

### Core Technologies

- **Language**: Java 11
- **Build Tool**: Maven 3.6+
- **UI Framework**: Swing
- **UI Theme**: FlatLaf 3.5
- **Database**: H2 2.4.240 + MyBatis 3.5.19

### Dependencies

| Dependency        | Version          | Purpose                |
|-------------------|------------------|------------------------|
| flatlaf           | 3.5              | Modern UI theme        |
| fesod-sheet       | 2.0.1-incubating | Excel streaming        |
| log4j-core        | 2.25.4           | Logging                |
| log4j-slf4j2-impl | 2.25.4           | SLF4J binding          |
| slf4j-api         | 2.0.16           | Logging API            |
| lombok            | 1.18.42          | Code simplification    |
| fastjson2         | 2.0.59           | JSON processing        |
| h2                | 2.4.240          | H2 Database            |
| mybatis           | 3.5.19           | MyBatis ORM            |
| miglayout-swing   | 11.3             | Layout manager         |
| simple-java-mail  | 8.12.6           | Email sending via SMTP |

## Design Patterns

SwissKit employs several design patterns:

### Plugin Pattern

```java
public interface KitPage { }
public class ExcelPage implements KitPage { }
```

### Observer Pattern

```java
public interface QueryAllEmailInfoCallBack {
    void onSuccess(List<EmailAddressBookEntity> result);
    void onFailure(Exception e);
}
```

### Factory Pattern

```java
public class UIUtils {
    public static JPanel createSectionPanel(String title, JComponent content) { }
    public static JLabel createPageTitle(String title) { }
}
```

### Module Pattern

```java
// SwissKitJ-Api module provides shared components
// Main module depends on API module
// Plugins also depend on API module for compatibility
```

---

**Ready to contribute?** Check out the [Development Guide](development.md) to start building!

1	# SwissKit
2	
3	![SwissKit](https://img.shields.io/badge/SwissKit-Desktop%20Toolbox-blue) ![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-11-orange) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green) ![Maven](https://img.shields.io/badge/Maven-3.6+-red)
4	
5	**SwissKit** is a *modular desktop toolbox* built with Java Swing. It provides a clean, extensible platform for various
6	utility tools including Excel file processing, email sending, email address book management, and plugin support. The
7	application uses a plugin-based architecture with automatic page discovery, making it easy to add new functionality.
8	
9	If you want a lightweight, fast, and customizable desktop utility suite, this is it.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Quick Start
14	
15	**Requirements:**
16	
17	- **JDK 11 or higher**
18	- **Maven 3.6 or higher**
19	
20	### Installation
21	
22	```bash
23	# Clone the repository
24	git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
25	cd SwissKitJ
26	
27	# Install API module first (required)
28	mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
29	
30	# Build the main project
31	mvn clean package
32	
33	# Run the application (executable JAR)
34	java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
35	```
36	
37	Or using Maven exec plugin:
38	
39	```bash
40	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
41	```
42	
43	### Development Mode
44	
45	```bash
46	# Compile
47	mvn clean compile
48	
49	# Run
50	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
51	```
52	
53	---
54	
55	## Features
56	
57	- **📦 Modular Architecture** - Plugin-based design with SPI auto-discovery
58	- **🎨 Modern UI** - Built with Swing and FlatLaf theme framework
59	- **⚡ High Performance** - Uses Apache FESOD for efficient Excel processing
60	- **🔄 Async Processing** - Background tasks with SwingWorker for non-blocking UI
61	- **💾 Database Support** - H2 + MyBatis for persistent storage
62	- **📧 Email Management** - Address book with tags for contact organization
63	- **🔌 Plugin System** - Install external JAR plugins to extend functionality
64	- **🛠️ Easy Extension** - Add new tools by implementing the `KitPage` interface
65	- **📊 Custom UI Components** - Gradient progress bar, fixed-width combo box
66	- **🚀 Splash Screen** - Professional startup experience with loading indicator
67	
68	### Current Tools
69	
70	#### 📊 Excel Tool
71	
72	- **File Analysis** - Read Excel file structure and extract headers
73	- **File Splitting** - Split Excel files by sheets or column values
74	- **Complex Split Mode** - Advanced splitting with custom configuration
75	- **Config View** - View and edit split configurations
76	- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress updates with percentage display
77	- **Multi-Sheet Support** - Handle multiple sheets in a single file
78	- **Streaming Data Processing** - Efficient memory usage with Apache FESOD
79	
80	#### 📧 Email Tool
81	
82	- **Email Composition** - Compose emails with subject and body
83	- **Recipient Management** - Add multiple recipients (To, Cc)
84	- **Mass Email Support** - Send emails to multiple contacts based on tags
85	- **Tag-based Recipients** - Load recipients from address book by tags
86	- **Attachment by Tag** - Attach files from tag-based folder selection
87	- **SMTP Integration** - Full SMTP support with TLS/SSL
88	- **Sent Log Viewer** - View history of sent emails with status tracking
89	
90	#### ⚙️ Settings
91	
92	- **Email Server Configuration** - SMTP settings with TLS/SSL support
93	- **Email Address Book** - Manage email contacts with nicknames; double-click to edit
94	- **Tag Management** - Create and manage tags for categorizing contacts
95	- **Plugin Installation** - Install JAR plugins via file upload
96	
97	#### 🏠 Welcome Page
98	
99	- Application overview and guidance
100	- Quick access to all tools
101	
102	#### 🎓 HappyLearning Plugin
103	
104	- **Auto Learning** - Automated online learning with configurable passkey
105	- **Config Upload** - Upload and manage learning configuration files
106	- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress bars for major and elective subjects
107	- **Start/Stop Control** - Manual control over learning sessions
108	- **Background Processing** - Non-blocking UI with SwingWorker
109	
110	---
111	
112	## Architecture
113	
114	### Project Modules
115	
116	SwissKit uses a multi-module Maven structure:
117	
118	| Module                 | Description                                                             |
119	|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
120	| `SwissKitJ-Api`        | Shared API module containing interfaces, annotations, and UI components |
121	| `SwissKit` (main)      | Core application with Excel, Email, and Settings tools                  |
122	| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning` | Auto-learning plugin with progress tracking     |
123	
124	### Project Structure
125	
126	```
127	SwissKitJ/                          # Project root
128	├── SwissKitJ-Api/                  # Shared API module
129	│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
130	│       ├── annoattion/
131	│       │   └── SwissKitPage.java
132	│       ├── api/
133	│       │   └── KitPage.java
134	│       └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/
135	│           ├── GradientProgressBar.java
136	│           └── FixedWidthComboBox.java
137	├── SwissKit/                       # Main application
138	│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
139	│       ├── Main.java
140	│       ├── database/               # H2 + MyBatis
141	│       ├── kitpage/                # Excel, Email, Settings pages
142	│       ├── plugin/                 # PluginLoader, IsolatedPluginClassLoader
143	│       ├── scaner/                 # SwissKitPageScaner (SPI)
144	│       ├── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/ # UI components
145	│       └── utils/                  # ExcelUtil, EmailUtil, etc.
146	├── OfficalPlugin/
147	│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/  # Built-in plugin
148	└── docs/                           # Documentation
149	```
150	
151	### Plugin System
152	
153	SwissKit uses an automatic discovery mechanism with annotations and SPI (Service Provider Interface) for tool pages:
154	
155	1. **Interface**: Implement the `KitPage` interface (from `fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
156	2. **Annotation**: Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation (from `fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage`)
157	3. **SPI Registration**: Add class name to `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
158	4. **Auto-discovery**: Pages are automatically discovered at runtime using `SwissKitPageScaner`
159	5. **Sorting**: Pages are sorted by `order()` value in annotation
160	
161	Example:
162	
163	```java
164	package fan.summer.kitpage.mytool;
165	
166	import fan.summer.api.KitPage;
167	import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage;
168	
169	import javax.swing.*;
170	
171	@SwissKitPage(
172	        menuName = "🔧 My Tool",
173	        menuTooltip = "Open My Tool",
174	        visible = true,
175	        order = 10
176	)
177	public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
178	  private JPanel panel;
179	
180	  public MyToolPage() {
181	    initComponents();
182	  }
183	
184	  private void initComponents() {
185	    panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
186	    JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
187	    titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
188	    panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
189	  }
190	
191	  @Override
192	  public JPanel getPanel() {
193	    return panel;
194	  }
195	}
196	```
197	
198	### Tech Stack
199	
200	| Category                | Technology       | Version          |
201	|-------------------------|------------------|------------------|
202	| **Language**            | Java             | 11               |
203	| **Build Tool**          | Maven            | 3.6+             |
204	| **UI Framework**        | Swing + FlatLaf  | 3.5              |
205	| **Excel Processing**    | Apache FESOD     | 2.0.1-incubating |
206	| **Database**            | H2 + MyBatis     | 2.4.240 / 3.5.19 |
207	| **Logging**             | Log4j2 + SLF4J   | 2.25.3 / 2.0.16  |
208	| **JSON**                | FastJSON2        | 2.0.59           |
209	| **Code Simplification** | Lombok           | 1.18.42          |
210	| **Email**               | Simple Java Mail | 8.12.6           |
211	
212	---
213	
214	## Database
215	
216	### Tables
217	
218	| Table Name                | Purpose                                    |
219	|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
220	| `swiss_kit_setting_email` | Email SMTP configuration                   |
221	| `complex_split_config`    | Excel complex split configuration          |
222	| `email_address_book`      | Email contacts with nicknames and tags     |
223	| `email_tag`               | Tags for categorizing email contacts       |
224	| `email_mass_sent_config`  | Mass email sending configuration           |
225	| `email_sent_log`          | Email sending history with status tracking |
226	
227	### Database Location
228	
229	- **Path**: `.swisskit/swisskit.db` (relative to application runtime directory)
230	- **Type**: H2 (embedded, file-based, no external server required)
231	
232	---
233	
234	## Development
235	
236	### Adding a New Tool
237	
238	1. Create a new package under `fan.summer.kitpage` (e.g., `pdf/`, `image/`)
239	2. Create a class implementing `KitPage` (from `SwissKitJ-Api` module)
240	3. Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation for menu configuration
241	4. Register in SPI plugin.swisskit.hpl.service file (`META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
242	5. The tool will be automatically discovered
243	
244	### Creating a Plugin
245	
246	1. Create a new Maven project with `SwissKitJ-Api` as dependency:

# SwissKit

![SwissKit](https://img.shields.io/badge/SwissKit-Desktop%20Toolbox-blue) ![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-11-orange) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green) ![Maven](https://img.shields.io/badge/Maven-3.6+-red)

**SwissKit** is a *modular desktop toolbox* built with Java Swing. It provides a clean, extensible platform for various
utility tools including Excel file processing, email sending, email address book management, and plugin support. The
application uses a plugin-based architecture with automatic page discovery, making it easy to add new functionality.

If you want a lightweight, fast, and customizable desktop utility suite, this is it.

---

## Quick Start

**Requirements:**

- **JDK 11 or higher**
- **Maven 3.6 or higher**

### Installation

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKitJ

# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build the main project
mvn clean package

# Run the application (executable JAR)
java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
```

Or using Maven exec plugin:

```bash
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

### Development Mode

```bash
# Compile
mvn clean compile

# Run
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

---

## Features

- **📦 Modular Architecture** - Plugin-based design with SPI auto-discovery
- **🎨 Modern UI** - Built with Swing and FlatLaf theme framework
- **⚡ High Performance** - Uses Apache FESOD for efficient Excel processing
- **🔄 Async Processing** - Background tasks with SwingWorker for non-blocking UI
- **💾 Database Support** - H2 + MyBatis for persistent storage
- **📧 Email Management** - Address book with tags for contact organization
- **🔌 Plugin System** - Install external JAR plugins to extend functionality
- **🛠️ Easy Extension** - Add new tools by implementing the `KitPage` interface
- **📊 Custom UI Components** - Gradient progress bar, fixed-width combo box
- **🚀 Splash Screen** - Professional startup experience with loading indicator

### Current Tools

#### 📊 Excel Tool

- **File Analysis** - Read Excel file structure and extract headers
- **File Splitting** - Split Excel files by sheets or column values
- **Complex Split Mode** - Advanced splitting with custom configuration
- **Config View** - View and edit split configurations
- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress updates with percentage display
- **Multi-Sheet Support** - Handle multiple sheets in a single file
- **Streaming Data Processing** - Efficient memory usage with Apache FESOD

#### 📧 Email Tool

- **Email Composition** - Compose emails with subject and body
- **Recipient Management** - Add multiple recipients (To, Cc)
- **Mass Email Support** - Send emails to multiple contacts based on tags
- **Tag-based Recipients** - Load recipients from address book by tags
- **Attachment by Tag** - Attach files from tag-based folder selection
- **SMTP Integration** - Full SMTP support with TLS/SSL
- **Sent Log Viewer** - View history of sent emails with status tracking

#### ⚙️ Settings

- **Email Server Configuration** - SMTP settings with TLS/SSL support
- **Email Address Book** - Manage email contacts with nicknames; double-click to edit
- **Tag Management** - Create and manage tags for categorizing contacts
- **Plugin Installation** - Install JAR plugins via file upload

#### 🏠 Welcome Page

- Application overview and guidance
- Quick access to all tools

#### 🎓 HappyLearning Plugin

- **Auto Learning** - Automated online learning with configurable passkey
- **Config Upload** - Upload and manage learning configuration files
- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress bars for major and elective subjects
- **Start/Stop Control** - Manual control over learning sessions
- **Background Processing** - Non-blocking UI with SwingWorker

---

## Architecture

### Project Modules

SwissKit uses a multi-module Maven structure:

| Module                 | Description                                                             |
|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `SwissKitJ-Api`        | Shared API module containing interfaces, annotations, and UI components |
| `SwissKit` (main)      | Core application with Excel, Email, and Settings tools                  |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning` | Auto-learning plugin with progress tracking     |

### Project Structure

```
SwissKitJ/                          # Project root
├── SwissKitJ-Api/                  # Shared API module
│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
│       ├── annoattion/
│       │   └── SwissKitPage.java
│       ├── api/
│       │   └── KitPage.java
│       └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/
│           ├── GradientProgressBar.java
│           └── FixedWidthComboBox.java
├── SwissKit/                       # Main application
│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
│       ├── Main.java
│       ├── database/               # H2 + MyBatis
│       ├── kitpage/                # Excel, Email, Settings pages
│       ├── plugin/                 # PluginLoader, IsolatedPluginClassLoader
│       ├── scaner/                 # SwissKitPageScaner (SPI)
│       ├── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/ # UI components
│       └── utils/                  # ExcelUtil, EmailUtil, etc.
├── OfficalPlugin/
│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/  # Built-in plugin
└── docs/                           # Documentation
```

### Plugin System

SwissKit uses an automatic discovery mechanism with annotations and SPI (Service Provider Interface) for tool pages:

1. **Interface**: Implement the `KitPage` interface (from `fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
2. **Annotation**: Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation (from `fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage`)
3. **SPI Registration**: Add class name to `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
4. **Auto-discovery**: Pages are automatically discovered at runtime using `SwissKitPageScaner`
5. **Sorting**: Pages are sorted by `order()` value in annotation

Example:

```java
package fan.summer.kitpage.mytool;

import fan.summer.api.KitPage;
import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage;

import javax.swing.*;

@SwissKitPage(
        menuName = "🔧 My Tool",
        menuTooltip = "Open My Tool",
        visible = true,
        order = 10
)
public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
  private JPanel panel;

  public MyToolPage() {
    initComponents();
  }

  private void initComponents() {
    panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
    JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
    titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
    panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
  }

  @Override
  public JPanel getPanel() {
    return panel;
  }
}
```

### Tech Stack

| Category                | Technology       | Version          |
|-------------------------|------------------|------------------|
| **Language**            | Java             | 11               |
| **Build Tool**          | Maven            | 3.6+             |
| **UI Framework**        | Swing + FlatLaf  | 3.5              |
| **Excel Processing**    | Apache FESOD     | 2.0.1-incubating |
| **Database**            | H2 + MyBatis     | 2.4.240 / 3.5.19 |
| **Logging**             | Log4j2 + SLF4J   | 2.25.3 / 2.0.16  |
| **JSON**                | FastJSON2        | 2.0.59           |
| **Code Simplification** | Lombok           | 1.18.42          |
| **Email**               | Simple Java Mail | 8.12.6           |

---

## Database

### Tables

| Table Name                | Purpose                                    |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| `swiss_kit_setting_email` | Email SMTP configuration                   |
| `complex_split_config`    | Excel complex split configuration          |
| `email_address_book`      | Email contacts with nicknames and tags     |
| `email_tag`               | Tags for categorizing email contacts       |
| `email_mass_sent_config`  | Mass email sending configuration           |
| `email_sent_log`          | Email sending history with status tracking |

### Database Location

- **Path**: `.swisskit/swisskit.db` (relative to application runtime directory)
- **Type**: H2 (embedded, file-based, no external server required)

---

## Development

### Adding a New Tool

1. Create a new package under `fan.summer.kitpage` (e.g., `pdf/`, `image/`)
2. Create a class implementing `KitPage` (from `SwissKitJ-Api` module)
3. Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation for menu configuration
4. Register in SPI plugin.swisskit.hpl.service file (`META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
5. The tool will be automatically discovered

### Creating a Plugin

1. Create a new Maven project with `SwissKitJ-Api` as dependency:
   ```xml
   <dependency>
       <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
       <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
       <version>2.0.1</version>
   </dependency>
   ```
2. Implement `KitPage` interface
3. Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation
4. Register in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
5. Package as JAR and install via Settings page

### Code Style

- Use camelCase for method and variable names
- Use PascalCase for class names
- Add Javadoc comments for all public methods
- All UI text and comments should be in English
- Use SLF4J Logger for logging
- Use `SansSerif` font for UI components

### Building

```bash
# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Clean build with executable JAR
mvn clean package

# Skip tests
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run executable JAR
java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
```

---

## Roadmap

- [x] Excel file analysis functionality
- [x] Excel file split by sheet functionality
- [x] Excel file split by column functionality
- [x] Excel complex split mode with configuration
- [x] Email address book management
- [x] Email tag management
- [x] Plugin installation support
- [x] Email sending with SMTP support
- [x] Mass email sending with tag-based recipients
- [x] Email sent log viewing functionality
- [ ] Add PDF processing tool
- [ ] Add image processing tool
- [x] Support theme switching
- [ ] Add unit tests
- [x] Add multi-language support

---

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please follow these guidelines:

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature`)
3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m ':sparkles: Add some AmazingFeature'`)
4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/AmazingFeature`)
5. Open a Pull Request

### Commit Message Format

Follow the conventional commits format with emojis:

- `✨` / `:sparkles:` - New feature
- `📝` / `:memo:` - Documentation
- `🐛` / `:bug:` - Bug fix
- `♻️` / `:recycle:` - Refactor code
- `⬆️` / `:arrow_up:` - Upgrade dependency

---

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.

---

## Authors

- **Summer** - Core development
- **PhoebeJ** - Email & Excel functionality

---

## Documentation

For detailed technical documentation, see [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md).

For user documentation, see the [docs](docs/) folder.

---

**Built with ❤️ using Java and Swing**

# Getting Started

Welcome to SwissKit! This guide will help you install, configure, and run SwissKit on your system.

## Requirements

Before you begin, ensure you have the following installed:

- **JDK 11 or higher**
- **Maven 3.6 or higher** (required for building from source)

### Verify Installation

```bash
# Check Java version
java -version

# Check Maven version
mvn -version
```

## Installation

### Option 1: Download Pre-built JAR

The easiest way to get started is to download the pre-built JAR file from the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/releases) page.

1. Download `SwissKit-2.1.1.jar`
2. Run the application:
   ```bash
   java -jar SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
   ```

### Option 2: Build from Source

If you want to build from source, follow these steps:

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKitJ

# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build the main project
mvn clean package

# Run the application
java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
```

## Configuration

### Application Settings

SwissKit comes with sensible defaults, but you can customize certain aspects:

#### Window Settings

Default window settings:
- **Minimum Size**: 800x500 pixels
- **Default Size**: 900x600 pixels
- **Theme**: FlatIntelliJLaf

#### Custom Icon

Place an icon file in `src/main/resources/`:
- `icon.png` (preferred)
- `icon.jpg`
- `app.png`

If not found, a log message will be displayed.

## Running the Application

### Using Maven Exec Plugin

```bash
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

### Using JAR File

```bash
java -jar target/SwissKit-2.0.2.jar
```

### Using IDE

If you're using IntelliJ IDEA:

1. Open the project
2. Locate `Main.java` in `src/main/java/fan/summer/`
3. Right-click and select "Run 'Main.main()'"

## First Steps

Once SwissKit is running:

1. **Splash Screen** - You'll see a loading screen while the application initializes
2. **Welcome Page** - After loading, you'll see the welcome page with an overview
3. **Select a Tool** - Choose a tool from the left sidebar:
   - **Excel Tool** - Process and split Excel files
   - **Email Tool** - Compose and send emails, view sent history
4. **Explore** - Click around to explore the available features

## Troubleshooting

### Application Won't Start

**Issue**: Application fails to start

**Solution**:
- Ensure JDK 11+ is installed
- Check `JAVA_HOME` environment variable
- Verify you're using the correct Java version

```bash
# Check Java version
java -version
```

### Excel Files Not Reading

**Issue**: Excel files are not being read properly

**Solution**:
- Ensure file format is supported (.xlsx, .xls)
- Check file permissions
- Verify the file is not corrupted

### UI Not Rendering Correctly

**Issue**: User interface appears broken or misaligned

**Solution**:
- Update FlatLaf dependencies
- Rebuild the project
- Try running with a fresh build

```bash
mvn clean package
```

### API Module Not Found

**Issue**: Build fails with "SwissKitJ-Api not found"

**Solution**:
- Install the API module first:
  ```bash
  mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
  ```

## Next Steps

Now that you have SwissKit running, you can:

- [Explore Features](features.md) - Learn about available tools
- [Read Architecture](architecture.md) - Understand how it works
- [Start Developing](development.md) - Contribute to the project

## Getting Help

If you encounter any issues:

1. Check the [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) section above
2. Search existing [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/issues)
3. Create a new issue with details about your problem

---

**Ready to explore?** Continue to [Features](features.md) to learn what SwissKit can do!

1	# Features
2	
3	SwissKit provides a variety of tools for everyday tasks. This section covers all available features and how to use them.
4	
5	## Table of Contents
6	
7	- [Excel Tool](#excel-tool)
8	- [Email Tool](#email-tool)
9	- [Settings](#settings)
10	- [Welcome Page](#welcome-page)
11	- [Plugin System](#plugin-system)
12	- [Custom UI Components](#custom-plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui-components)
13	
14	## Excel Tool
15	
16	The Excel tool provides powerful file processing capabilities including analysis and splitting.
17	
18	### File Analysis
19	
20	Analyze Excel files to extract structure and header information.
21	
22	**Features**:
23	
24	- Read all sheet pages
25	- Extract headers from each sheet
26	- Real-time progress display
27	- Support for .xlsx and .xls formats
28	
29	**How to Use**:
30	
31	1. Click the "Select File" button
32	2. Choose an Excel file
33	3. Click "Analyze" button
34	4. View results in the progress area
35	
36	**Example Output**:
37	
38	```
39	Parsing completed! Total 3 sheets
40	- Sheet1: [ID, Name, Email, Phone]
41	- Sheet2: [Date, Amount, Status]
42	- Sheet3: [Product, Quantity, Price]
43	```
44	
45	### File Splitting
46	
47	Split Excel files by sheet into separate files.
48	
49	**Features**:
50	
51	- Split each sheet into individual Excel file
52	- Real-time progress updates with percentage
53	- Automatic header preservation
54	- Warning dialogs for validation
55	
56	**How to Use**:
57	
58	1. Select an Excel file
59	2. Choose output directory
60	3. Select "Split by Sheet" checkbox
61	4. Click "Split" button
62	5. Wait for completion
63	
64	**Output Structure**:
65	
66	```
67	output/
68	├── Sheet1.xlsx
69	├── Sheet2.xlsx
70	└── Sheet3.xlsx
71	```
72	
73	### Complex Split Mode
74	
75	Advanced splitting with custom configuration stored in database.
76	
77	**Features**:
78	
79	- Custom header row index
80	- Custom split column index
81	- Configuration persistence
82	- Edit saved configurations

343	- **Config Upload** - Upload learning configuration files (netschool-headers.json)
344	- **Auto Learning** - Automated learning with configurable lesson type
345	- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress bars for major and elective subjects
